mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p<0.05, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which improves short-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO acceleration, or the mechanism reverses direction during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate income, which supports near-term equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of payroll growth months, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms robust economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of high-growth quarters, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (p>0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy response that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns, or 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and slowing output growth, which erodes corporate profit forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production declines, or the signal loses significance (p>0.10) in late-cycle subsamples.
